title: Chaturdasha Temple
tagline: Chaturdasha Temple — spiritual town of Tripura

overview.short: Chaturdasha Temple is a Hindu temple in Agartala, Tripura, India, and features the Tripuri dome patterned after the roofs of village huts in Tripura.
overview.description: Chaturdasha Temple is a Hindu temple in Agartala, Tripura, India, and features the Tripuri dome patterned after the roofs of village huts in Tripura. The dome is surmounted by a stupa-like structure which reveals traces of Buddhist influence. This temple was built in honour of fourteen deities, together called the Chaturdasha Devata. Devotees visit the temple for the kharchi festival.

topPlaces.name: Ranirbazar
topPlaces.description: Ranirbazar is a town and a Municipal Council in West Tripura district in the Indian state of Tripura.

topPlaces.name: Jogendranagar
topPlaces.description: Jogendranagar is a census town in West Tripura district in the Indian state of Tripura. Jogendranagar has the only railway station after Agartala railway station, in Agartala.

topPlaces.name: Dhaleshwar
topPlaces.description: Dhaleshwar is a locality in Agartala, Tripura close to Banamalipur, Indra Nagar, Kalyani and Math Chowhamani. It comes under East Police Station and Dhaleswar Post Office.

topPlaces.name: Swami Vivekananda Stadium
topPlaces.description: Swami Vivekananda Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium in the capital city of Tripura in Agartala. The ground has capacity of 8,000 persons.

topPlaces.name: Indranagar
topPlaces.description: Indranagar is a census town in West Tripura district in the Indian state of Tripura. The locality is famous for the Kali temple.

topPlaces.name: Banamalipur
topPlaces.description: Banamalipur is a locality in Agartala, Tripura, India.

topPlaces.name: Pratapgarh, Tripura
topPlaces.description: Pratapgarh is a census town in West Tripura district in the Indian state of Tripura.

topPlaces.name: Abhoynagar
topPlaces.description: Abhoynagar, also known as Abhaynagar, Abhoinagar, Abhainagar, is a town in Agartala, Tripura, India. Meitei people in Tripura mainly worship their traditional deity Puthiba in this place, and celebrate state level Lai Haraoba in Tripura, which is recognised by the Government of Tripura as an official state holiday.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chaturdasha Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chaturdasha Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chaturdasha Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharaja Bir Bikram Airport, Agartala (12 km) and the nearest railway station is Agartala Railway Station (9 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chaturdasha Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chaturdasha Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chaturdasha Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chaturdasha Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chaturdasha Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ranirbazar, Jogendranagar, Dhaleshwar, Swami Vivekananda Stadium, Indranagar.
